sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Athletic Club have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, indicating inconsistency but a knack for scoring. Levante UD, on the other hand, have struggled with 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss, though their recent 3-2 win over Elche and 3-0 victory against Sevilla suggest potential for upsets. Both teams have defensive vulnerabilities, with Athletic conceding in all of their last 10 games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, Athletic Club have dominated Levante UD, securing a 2-0 away win in November 2025 and a 3-1 home victory in March 2022. Athletic remain unbeaten in their last 10 matches against Levante, which gives them a significant psychological edge heading into this match.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Athletic Club (11th, 25 points) outperform Levante UD (19th, 18 points) in key areas like penalty area entries and final third pressing, ranking 1st in the league for both. However, their defense has been leaky, conceding 31 goals compared to Levante's 34. Levante’s attack has shown sparks with players like Etta Eyong (6 goals), but their overall consistency remains a concern.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Athletic’s Nico Williams, with a strong expected goals tally (4.44) and dribbling rank (4th in the league), could be a game-changer, while Robert Navarro is in form with 2 goals and 2 assists in recent matches. For Levante, Etta Eyong’s finishing (6 goals) and Iván Romero’s ability to draw fouls (2.7 per game, 4th in league) might test Athletic’s shaky defense.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Athletic Club’s historical dominance over Levante UD and their superior league standing, coupled with Sofascore’s 51% home win probability, the hosts are favored to win. Levante’s recent attacking output keeps them in contention for a goal, but their defensive frailties and poor away form tilt the balance. Expect a competitive match with goals on both sides, but Athletic should edge it at San Mamés.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Athletic Club leads Levante UD 2-0 at San Mamés in Bilbao. The home side has dominated the scoring with two well-taken goals by Gorka Guruzeta, showcasing their clinical finishing. Levante UD, on the other hand, finds themselves in a challenging position, compounded by a red card that leaves them a man down for the second half. The first half saw Athletic Club capitalize on their opportunities while Levante struggled to convert their chances into meaningful threats.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Athletic Club's dominance
halftimeSections.text: Athletic Club has been the superior side in the opening 45 minutes. Their ability to create big chances and convert them into goals has been evident, with Guruzeta leading the charge. The home team's entries into the final third and penalty area have been frequent, allowing them to maintain pressure on Levante's defense. Their aerial prowess has also given them an edge in physical battles, which could be crucial as the game progresses.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Levante UD's struggles
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD has found it tough to cope with Athletic Club's intensity in the first half. Despite having a slight edge in ball possession, their attacking output has been minimal, with no shots on target to show for their efforts. The red card further complicates their situation, reducing their chances of mounting a comeback. Their defense has been under constant pressure, and without numerical parity, maintaining discipline at the back will be a significant challenge.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: Throughout the first half, Athletic Club consistently maintained attacking momentum with frequent spells of pressure in the opposition's half. Their attacking intent was clear from the early minutes, with sustained periods of dominance that led to their two goals. Levante UD had brief moments of momentum, particularly towards the end of the half, but failed to create any real danger. The momentum heavily favored the home side for the majority of the half, with only short-lived shifts towards the away team.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Athletic Club, Gorka Guruzeta has been the standout performer, scoring both goals and posing a constant threat upfront. Iñaki Williams has also been influential with his creativity, contributing to the build-up play. On the Levante side, Iván Romero showed some promise but missed a big chance, which might weigh on their confidence. With a man down, Levante will need a heroic effort from their remaining players to turn things around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Athletic Club is likely to continue their aggressive approach, exploiting Levante's numerical disadvantage. They will aim to control the game and potentially add to their tally with their attacking players in fine form. Levante UD, meanwhile, will need to focus on defensive organization and look for counter-attacking opportunities. However, their chances of scoring seem slim given their lack of cutting edge in the first half and the challenge of playing with ten men.
